title: Truly Modular: Armory
description: An Addon for Truly Modular focusing on Armor and Elytras
body:
# **Truly Modular: Armory**  
*A wide Collections of Modular Armor Modules*
  
![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6mUV27DN/images/677e5a34444926fc2e8220fb5983a794afdb743f.png)
  

Truly Modular: Armory is a Modular Armor Mod with wide ranging support and options
     
![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6mUV27DN/images/5acdbdf1ce7fc723e5235d88b65096c71b580a87.png)  

## **Contents**
- more then 60 Modules split between base sets
- Crafted Chainmail: a good armor against Projectile Damage
- Plate Armor: the default Armor
- Heavy Armor: Heavy Armor, excelent Protection, but moving with Elytra or swimming is difficult with it.
- Wingsuit: Not much in the way of Protection, but allows for excelent Elytra Performance
- Scuba: not the best in terms of Protection, but increased Swimspeed
- Multiple visual/cosmetic options like Banners
![](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6mUV27DN/images/09ac8decc8fd798bc2aef9b457bd84d4eb7dd6b5.png)
- Truly Modular:Armory is balanced arround providing similar armor values than vanilla, but those are often less than sensible. We recommend the usage of [Armor Curve](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/armor-curve) or similar mods to make vanillas armorscalling more sensible


## **Compat**
- [Better Combat](https://modrinth.com/mod/better-combat) is fully supported
- Truly Modular uses a Automatic compat by default, this means most mods Swords and Materials should get picked up, dedicated compat is possible through datapacks.   
You can use the [Material Helper Website](https://truly-modular.github.io/Material-Helper/) to make compat datapacks yourself

## **FAQ:**
 - What Launcher is this for? Forge? Fabric?
   - Both, check the jars
 - Backport to older versions?
    - is not planned. There are many features in Truly Modular witch have a significantly higher priority than backporting. Once the list of ideas is smaller we might consider backporting.
  - is X mod supported?
    - probably? Generative Compatiblity has a high chance of working with most mods out of the box. You can check [here](https://github.com/Truly-Modular/Modular-Item-API/blob/main/COMPAT.md) for more informations or to report issues
 - Can i write a Addon for this?
   - Yes, for any help feel free to visit our [discord](https://discord.gg/m9AbjqvcGn) or check the API [wiki](https://github.com/Truly-Modular/Modular-Item-API/wiki) for additional Help
 - Can i use this in a Modpack?
   - yes
